notes: Image is straightforward minimalist logo — Gemma models (12b/26b/31b/e4b) and GLM flash all performed strongly, identifying all three symbolic elements (heart, hands, laurel wreath) and correct verbatim text 'VIC Health Club'.
Qwen 3.6 27b (both think and nothink variants) achieved perfect scores — best in class.

Qwen models generally struggled with text OCR: 'VIC' was misread as 'Civic' (qwen3-vl-4b), 'WIC' (qwen3.5-4b x2), 'Vivo' (qwen3.5-9b nothink), 'VIM' (qwen3.6-35b nothink), 'Vik' (qwen3.6-35b think). This is a striking pattern of V/C confusion in Qwen variants.
qwen3.5-9b|think returned an EMPTY response — thinking-phase runaway failure, scored 0 per instructions.
qwen3.5-4b|nothink produced an extended hallucination about the WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) government program — severe over-interpretation.
qwen3.5-9b|nothink invented 'Vivo Health Hub' — both the brand name AND the suffix ('Health Club' → 'Health Hub') were wrong.
